title: [Bug]: NatsEnvelope preserves control characters in header names
summary: NatsEnvelope header normalization currently allows header names containing ASCII control characters.
observed: Creating an envelope with a header key containing a control character preserves that key in envelope.headers.
expected: Malformed header names containing control characters should be discarded at the envelope boundary.
reproduction: Create NatsEnvelope(headers={'Bad\u0000Header': 'value'}) and observe that the malformed key remains.
failing_test: Add a unit test that verifies control-character-bearing header names are removed from normalized envelope headers.
impact: Control characters in persisted header metadata can affect logs, terminal output, and downstream inspection tools.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix only normalizes malformed metadata.
security: The fix must avoid echoing unsafe header names or values in logs.
acceptance: Header names containing ASCII control characters are omitted from envelope.headers.
Header values are not logged during normalization.
Valid headers continue to be preserved.
tests: Run focused envelope tests and full validation.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
